steps.title: Use the contrast
steps.content: The word but sets up a contrast between what some economists assumed and what Bianchi's team found. The findings show that clustering factors affect some industries more strongly than others, so the earlier assumption must have been that the reasons were the same across sectors.

steps.title: Reject the wrong meanings
steps.content: Choice A means joined together, not the same across sectors. Choice B describes what Bianchi found, not what earlier economists assumed. Choice D means fake or unnatural, which the passage does not suggest. The answer is C.
